column.name: ClientDivision
column.description: Client segmentation or division tags for the customer outlet (e.g., "Parlour,Both,Hair"), often used for channel/segment analysis. Values may be null/blank, so treat it as an optional attribute rather than a reliable key.

column.name: Client_Id
column.description: Unique numeric identifier for the client/outlet, used to join to client masters and to filter the large heap table efficiently (indexed). Analysts should treat this as the primary client key; it is more stable than names.

column.name: Client_Name
column.description: Human-readable name of the client/outlet (e.g., store or parlour name). Use for reporting/display only; it may have casing/spacing variations and is not guaranteed unique.

column.name: Client_Type
column.description: Business classification of the client such as Retailer, Wholesale, Distributor, or Parlor. Useful for channel performance and inventory movement comparisons across customer types.

column.name: ClosingImpactC1
column.description: Closing stock adjustment/impact quantity in the C1 unit of measure (often the base/count unit), typically from audits or corrections. Usually 0; non-zero indicates post-calculation adjustments affecting closing quantity.

column.name: ClosingImpactValueC1
column.description: Monetary value corresponding to the ClosingImpactC1 adjustment. Use to quantify the financial impact of closing adjustments in the same valuation basis as other *ValueC1 fields.

column.name: Function
column.description: Additional client or product function/usage descriptor provided by source systems; often blank or "null". Treat as free-text/optional metadata and avoid using as a primary grouping without cleansing.

column.name: MRP
column.description: Maximum Retail Price for the SKU/variant as a numeric amount (e.g., 125.0, 310.0). This is a price reference for the item and may differ from the valuation used in the Value fields.

column.name: OpeningImpactC1
column.description: Opening stock adjustment/impact quantity in C1, representing corrections applied to the opening balance. Typically 0; non-zero suggests prior-period reconciliation or audit-driven adjustment.

column.name: OpeningImpactValueC1
column.description: Monetary value corresponding to the OpeningImpactC1 adjustment. Use to measure the financial effect of opening balance corrections.

column.name: PName
column.description: Concatenated or display product name often in the format "Product / Product" as received from the source. Useful for UI display but redundant with Product_Name and not ideal for deduplication.

column.name: ProDivision
column.description: Product/business division or line (e.g., Retail, Parlour, Both) indicating where the SKU is positioned organizationally. Use for high-level product portfolio reporting and division splits.

column.name: ProductDescription
column.description: Free-text description and/or extended attributes for the product, sometimes containing semi-structured key/value fragments. Content quality varies widely; use cautiously and consider parsing/standardization for analytics.

column.name: Product_Category
column.description: Category or sub-category label for the product (e.g., Aloe Soap, Gold Bleach 85g). Use for assortment/category performance; may be more granular than ProDivision.

column.name: Product_Id
column.description: Unique numeric identifier for the product (indexed), used for joins to product masters and efficient filtering. Interpret as the product-level key (may aggregate multiple variants).

column.name: Product_Name
column.description: Primary product name as maintained in the product catalog (e.g., "Lacto Bleach 50g"). Use for reporting labels; not guaranteed unique across pack sizes/variants without Variant_Id or VSKU_Code.

column.name: TotalAuditImpact
column.description: Net quantity impact from audit/reconciliation activities over the period (non-C1 unit). Typically 0; non-zero indicates audit-driven stock corrections.

column.name: TotalAuditImpactValue
column.description: Value associated with TotalAuditImpact, representing the financial impact of audit adjustments. Use alongside TotalAuditImpact to assess shrinkage/overage valuation.

column.name: TotalClosing
column.description: Closing stock quantity at end of the period for the client and SKU/variant; can be negative when data corrections or timing issues exist. Use as the ending inventory position and investigate negatives as exceptions.

column.name: TotalClosingC1
column.description: Closing stock quantity expressed in the C1/base unit of measure. Often matches TotalClosing in samples, but treat as the standardized quantity field for cross-item comparisons.

column.name: TotalClosingImpact
column.description: Total quantity impact applied to derive closing stock (non-C1), aggregating adjustment components beyond normal movements. Generally 0; non-zero indicates closing-balance corrections.

column.name: TotalClosingImpactValue
column.description: Value of TotalClosingImpact adjustments. Use to quantify the financial effect of closing corrections separately from normal stock movement valuation.

column.name: TotalClosingValue
column.description: Valuation of the closing stock quantity (currency amount), based on the system’s valuation logic (not necessarily MRP). Use for inventory value reporting at period end; may be negative if TotalClosing is negative.

column.name: TotalClosingValueC1
column.description: Valuation of the closing stock in the C1/base unit context. Typically aligns with TotalClosingValue; use as the standardized closing value where C1 is the reporting basis.

column.name: TotalDeletedSaleQty
column.description: Quantity of sales transactions that were deleted/voided during the period. Use to identify corrections to sales activity; typically 0 in normal operations.

column.name: TotalDeletedSaleValue
column.description: Value associated with deleted/voided sales quantities. Useful for audit trails and to reconcile net sales vs. gross activity.

column.name: TotalInterGodownTransfer
column.description: Quantity moved via inter-godown (warehouse-to-warehouse) transfers for the client/SKU over the period. Typically 0 at retail outlets; non-zero indicates inventory rebalancing across locations.

column.name: TotalInterGodownTransferValue
column.description: Valuation of inter-godown transfer quantities. Use for stock movement valuation reconciliation rather than revenue analysis.

column.name: TotalOpening
column.description: Opening stock quantity at start of the period for the client and SKU/variant; can be negative in exceptional cases. Use as the starting inventory position for movement reconciliation.

column.name: TotalOpeningC1
column.description: Opening stock quantity expressed in C1/base unit of measure. Use as the standardized opening quantity for comparisons and reconciliation across items.

column.name: TotalOpeningImpact
column.description: Total quantity impact applied to opening stock (non-C1), reflecting adjustments/corrections to the opening balance. Usually 0; non-zero indicates prior-period reconciliation effects.

column.name: TotalOpeningImpactValue
column.description: Value of TotalOpeningImpact adjustments. Use to quantify financial corrections applied to opening inventory.

column.name: TotalOpeningValue
column.description: Valuation of opening stock quantity (currency amount) using the system’s valuation basis. Use to report beginning inventory value and to support inventory roll-forward analyses.

column.name: TotalOpeningValueC1
column.description: Valuation of opening stock in the C1/base unit context. Use as the standardized opening value when C1 is the reporting basis.

column.name: TotalPurchaseReturn
column.description: Quantity of goods returned back to supplier/stock source during the period. Use to analyze reverse flows and procurement quality; typically 0 for many outlet-level records.

column.name: TotalPurchaseReturnValue
column.description: Valuation of purchase return quantities. Use to reconcile inventory value decreases due to supplier returns.

column.name: TotalRecieved
column.description: Total quantity received into stock during the period (inbound receipts). Interpret as replenishment/inward movement; spelling suggests it is sourced as-is from upstream systems.

column.name: TotalRecievedValue
column.description: Valuation of received quantities. Use for inward stock value tracking and reconciliation with procurement/invoice data where applicable.

column.name: TotalReturnRecieved
column.description: Quantity received as returns (e.g., customer returns or reverse receipts) during the period. Use to separate normal receipts from return-driven inflows.

column.name: TotalReturnRecievedValue
column.description: Valuation associated with return receipts. Useful for quantifying the value impact of returns entering inventory.

column.name: TotalSaleQty
column.description: Total sold quantity for the client and SKU/variant during the period. Use as the primary outbound demand measure; typically non-negative and may be 0 when no sales occurred.

column.name: TotalSaleValue
column.description: Value associated with TotalSaleQty, representing sales amount per the source valuation logic. Use for sales performance, noting it may not equal MRP * quantity due to discounts/taxes/valuation rules.

column.name: TotalStockIn
column.description: Total inbound stock movement quantity (broad stock-in bucket) during the period, potentially including receipts and other inbound adjustments. Use for operational movement analysis and compare with TotalRecieved to understand composition.

column.name: TotalStockInValue
column.description: Valuation of TotalStockIn movements. Use to reconcile inventory value increases due to inbound movements.

column.name: TotalStockOut
column.description: Total outbound stock movement quantity (broad stock-out bucket) during the period, potentially including sales and other outbound adjustments. Use with TotalSaleQty to distinguish demand from other outflows.

column.name: TotalStockOutIn
column.description: Quantity of stock moved out and then in (or an out-in transfer bucket) as captured by the source system. Typically 0; non-zero may indicate internal transfers/returns workflows and should be interpreted with business rules from the source.

column.name: TotalStockOutInValue
column.description: Valuation corresponding to TotalStockOutIn movements. Use for reconciliation of internal movement value that is not pure sale/receipt.

column.name: TotalStockOutValue
column.description: Valuation of TotalStockOut movements. Useful for inventory value roll-forward and to quantify value leaving stock via various outflow types.

column.name: TotalTarnsitQty
column.description: Quantity currently in transit or moved through transit during the period (spelled "Tarnsit" in source). Use to analyze pipeline inventory; typically 0 at outlet level unless transfers are tracked.

column.name: TotalTarnsitValue
column.description: Valuation associated with in-transit quantities. Use to quantify the value tied up in transit/pipeline stock.

column.name: VSKU_Code
column.description: Variant/SKU code used operationally (e.g., numeric codes or alphanumeric like "NR10617/01"), often the most recognizable SKU identifier for business users. Use for SKU-level reporting and as a join key to variant catalogs when Product_Id is too coarse.

column.name: Variant_Id
column.description: Unique numeric identifier for the product variant/SKU (indexed), representing the most granular item level in this table. Use as the primary key for variant-level joins and always prefer it over names for grouping/deduplication.

column.name: Zone
column.description: Geographical or sales zone assignment for the client/outlet (e.g., West, North, East-1). Use for regional rollups and territory performance comparisons; values reflect commercial zoning rather than administrative states.
